DEFENDANT ANITA MULCAHY APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER OFF CALENDAR, untimely CCP 1005; the earliest this motion could have been set for hearing, assuming personal service, was September 30, 2014. No request for judicial notice of August 26, 2014 filings was made and no courtesy copies of such filings have been provided (SFLR 2.6B). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARK ALLEN BARNES VS. SHIRLEY HOLMES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Expunge Ntc Of Pendency Of Action (Lis Pendens) And For Attorney Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 23, 2014 line 8. DEFENDANT SHIRLEY HOLMES' Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action (Lis Pendens) And For Attorney Fees And Costs DENIED. Plaintiff has shown probability of prevailing on his real property claim re: possession of the unit. Tenant has a right to possession of the premises until his tenancy is lawfully terminated. Carter v. Cohen (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 1038; Salazar v. Maradeaga (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th Supp. 1. Attorneys fees and costs granted in the amount of $2,000. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TIM CHOI SUN et al VS. ELAINE TAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint Third Cause Of Action - Anti-Slapp
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 23, 2014 line 9. DEFENDANT ELAINE TAN's Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint Third Cause Of Action - Anti-Slapp DENIED. Plaintiff's third cause of action does not arise out of protected activity. At most, the complaint arising out of repudiation of trust, is evidenced by the claim rejection filed in a probate proceeding. "The appropriate course of action when a creditor's claim is rejected is for the creditor to commence a separate action on the rejected claim challenging rejection of the creditor's claim. (Miller, supra, 15 Cal.App.2d at pp. 614-615, 59 P.2d 1035; §§ 9250, 9252.) This is the exclusive method of enforcing a claim which has been rejected by the court." McDonald v. Structured Asset Sales, LLC (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 1068. Hearing Required to address why sanctions should not be granted. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOEL ELLIOTT VS. MICHAEL GALLIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 23, 2014 line 13. DEFENDANTS MICHAEL GALLIN and AARON MARXMILLER's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of Service on file. Proof of Service indicates that Order Shortening Time and Motion to Vacate were served on 9/9/2014. However, Order Shortening Time was granted on 9/10/14 and Motion to Vacate was filed on 9/10/2014. Defendant's filing on September 22, 2014 is stricken. = (501/REQ)
